questions.q: What was the SLV-3?
questions.explanation: SLV-3 (Satellite Launch Vehicle-3) was India's first indigenous launch vehicle capable of placing a satellite into orbit.

questions.q: Which Indian space agency developed the SLV-3?
questions.explanation: The SLV-3 was designed and built by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO).

questions.q: Who was the project director of the SLV-3?
questions.explanation: A. P. J. Abdul Kalam served as the project director of the SLV-3 programme.

questions.q: Which satellite did the SLV-3 successfully place into orbit in 1980?
questions.explanation: The successful 1980 flight of the SLV-3 placed the Rohini (RS-1) satellite into orbit.

questions.q: In which year did the SLV-3 achieve its first successful orbital launch?
questions.explanation: The SLV-3 successfully placed a satellite into orbit on 18 July 1980.

questions.q: The success of the SLV-3 made India the member of which exclusive group of nations?
options: Nations capable of launching satellites into orbit
questions.explanation: With SLV-3's success, India joined the small group of nations able to launch a satellite using their own launch vehicle.

questions.q: What type of propulsion did all stages of the SLV-3 use?
questions.explanation: The SLV-3 was an all-solid-propellant rocket, using solid fuel motors in all of its stages.

questions.q: From which Indian launch site was the SLV-3 launched?
questions.explanation: The SLV-3 was launched from the Sriharikota Range (SHAR) on the east coast of India.

questions.q: How many stages did the SLV-3 launch vehicle have?
questions.explanation: The SLV-3 was a four-stage rocket, all using solid propellant.

questions.q: The SLV-3 was a milestone in which area of India's scientific effort?
questions.explanation: The SLV-3 was a landmark achievement in India's space and rocketry programme.

questions.q: On what date did the SLV-3 successfully orbit the Rohini satellite?
questions.explanation: The successful SLV-3 flight (designated SLV-3 E2) took place on 18 July 1980.

questions.q: What happened during the first experimental flight of the SLV-3 in August 1979?
options: It placed Rohini in a perfect orbit
It was a partial failure and did not achieve orbit
It exploded on the launch pad
questions.explanation: The first SLV-3 flight on 10 August 1979 (SLV-3 E1) failed to reach orbit due to a second-stage control problem; the satellite fell into the sea.

questions.q: Approximately how much payload could the SLV-3 carry to low Earth orbit?
questions.explanation: The SLV-3 could place a payload of roughly 40 kg into a low Earth orbit.

questions.q: Roughly how tall was the SLV-3 launch vehicle?
questions.explanation: The SLV-3 stood about 22 metres tall and weighed around 17 tonnes at liftoff.

questions.q: Which earlier US rocket did the four-stage solid SLV-3 broadly resemble in configuration?
questions.explanation: The SLV-3's four-stage, all-solid design was comparable in concept and class to the American Scout launch vehicle.

questions.q: Who was the chairman of ISRO who oversaw the SLV-3 programme to its success?
questions.explanation: Satish Dhawan was the ISRO chairman during the SLV-3 development and its successful 1980 flight.

questions.q: What was the primary purpose of building the SLV-3?
options: To demonstrate India's ability to launch its own satellites
To test long-range ballistic missiles only
To land a probe on the Moon
questions.explanation: The SLV-3's main goal was to develop and prove India's indigenous capability to place satellites into orbit.

questions.q: Which series of launch vehicles directly built upon the technology proven by the SLV-3?
questions.explanation: Experience from the SLV-3 led to the Augmented Satellite Launch Vehicle (ASLV) and ultimately the PSLV.

questions.q: The Rohini satellite launched by the SLV-3 was used mainly for what purpose?
questions.explanation: The Rohini satellite was an experimental payload used to gather technical and scientific data and validate the launch.

questions.q: How many flights did the SLV-3 programme conduct in total?
questions.explanation: The SLV-3 programme conducted four flights between 1979 and 1983, the first being a failure and later ones succeeding.

questions.q: Whose early vision of an indigenous Indian space programme set the stage for projects like the SLV-3?
questions.explanation: Vikram Sarabhai, the founder of ISRO, laid the foundations for India's space programme that produced the SLV-3.

questions.q: What does the abbreviation 'SLV' stand for?
questions.explanation: SLV stands for Satellite Launch Vehicle, indicating its purpose of orbiting satellites.

questions.q: What kind of orbit did the SLV-3 place the Rohini RS-1 satellite into in 1980?
questions.explanation: The SLV-3 placed Rohini RS-1 into a low Earth orbit, the only regime it was designed to reach.

questions.q: What was the chief technical reason the first SLV-3 flight (E1) in 1979 failed to reach orbit?
options: A second-stage control and guidance failure
The payload fairing failed to separate
questions.explanation: The 1979 maiden flight failed because of a problem in the second-stage control system, causing the vehicle to plunge into the sea.

questions.q: Approximately how much did the SLV-3 weigh at liftoff?
questions.explanation: The SLV-3 had a liftoff mass of roughly 17 tonnes, making it a comparatively small launch vehicle.

questions.q: After the success of the SLV-3, which augmented vehicle added strap-on boosters to increase payload capacity?
questions.explanation: The Augmented Satellite Launch Vehicle (ASLV) added strap-on boosters to the SLV-3 core to lift heavier payloads.

questions.q: Which two satellites were launched by the later, repeat flights of the SLV-3 after the 1980 success?
questions.explanation: The later SLV-3 development flights in 1981 and 1983 carried the Rohini RS-D1 and RS-D2 satellites.

questions.q: Which institution led the development of the SLV-3, where Abdul Kalam worked as project director?
questions.explanation: The SLV-3 was developed primarily at the Vikram Sarabhai Space Centre (VSSC) in Thiruvananthapuram, where Kalam led the project.

questions.q: The Rohini satellite series launched by the SLV-3 was named after what?
options: A star or asterism in Indian tradition
questions.explanation: Rohini is the traditional Indian name for a star (in the constellation Taurus), reflecting ISRO's habit of naming satellites after celestial objects.

questions.q: How did the SLV-3 experience benefit India's strategic missile programme?
options: It had no connection to missiles
Its solid-rocket and guidance know-how informed later missile development
It directly became the Agni-V missile
It was converted into a fighter jet engine
questions.explanation: The solid-propulsion and guidance expertise gained from the SLV-3 contributed to the technological base later used in India's missile programmes.
